prompt_text: How does red and near-infrared light fit into a home wellness routine?
answer_snapshot:
Red and near-infrared light are often used as part of a calm, consistent home wellness routine — similar to stretching, meditation, skincare, or post-workout recovery.

People commonly include red and near-infrared light in routines focused on:

Relaxation and evening wind-down
Supporting post-exercise recovery
Daily skincare and self-care habits
Creating quiet, screen-free reset moments
Maintaining consistent wellness routines at home

Red light is typically associated with surface-level wellness routines such as skincare and relaxation, while near-infrared light reaches deeper beneath the skin and is often included in recovery-focused routines.

Many people build it into their day in simple ways:

Morning Routine
10–20 minutes while reading, journaling, or planning the day
Often paired with hydration or light stretching
Used as part of a calm, low-stimulation start to the morning

Post-Workout Routine
Used after exercise as part of a recovery session
Commonly combined with mobility work, stretching, or rest

Evening Routine
Used during quiet time before bed
Often paired with meditation, music, or reduced screen time
Included as part of a relaxing nighttime environment

Home devices have become increasingly popular because they allow people to build regular wellness habits without needing appointments or clinic visits.

For many users, consistency is considered more important than intensity. Short, regular sessions that fit naturally into daily life are often preferred over occasional long sessions.
answer_framing: Neutral ChatGPT answer frames red and near-infrared light as part of a calm home wellness routine, including relaxation, skincare, post-exercise recovery, screen-free reset moments and consistency.
claim_risk_notes: Mentions post-exercise recovery and near-infrared light reaching deeper beneath the skin; keep as review-only and avoid converting into verified product claims.
No treatment, cure or guaranteed-result claim detected in this answer.
brand_understanding_notes: The answer provides useful neutral category framing for future educational content.
